skill.name: mcp-apps-builder
skill.description:
**MANDATORY for ALL MCP server work** - mcp-use framework best practices and patterns.

**READ THIS FIRST** before any MCP server work, including:
- Creating new MCP servers
- Modifying existing MCP servers (adding/updating tools, resources, prompts, widgets)
- Debugging MCP server issues or errors
- Reviewing MCP server code for quality, security, or performance
- Answering questions about MCP development or mcp-use patterns
- Making ANY changes to server.tool(), server.resource(), server.prompt(), or widgets

This skill contains critical architecture decisions, security patterns, and common pitfalls.
Always consult the relevant reference files BEFORE implementing MCP features.
skill.summary: Essential reference skill for MCP server development using the mcp-use framework
skill.author: mcp-use

security_audit.summary: All 1766 flagged patterns are false positives. The findings are in markdown documentation files (SKILL.md, references/*.md) containing code examples and tutorials. No executable code with security implications detected. This is a documentation/reference skill for mcp-use framework best practices.

content.user_title: Build MCP Servers with mcp-use Framework
content.value_statement: Developers building MCP servers need guidance on framework best practices, security patterns, and common implementation pitfalls. This skill provides comprehensive documentation and reference materials for creating production-ready MCP servers using the mcp-use framework.
actual_capabilities: Provides reference documentation for MCP server architecture and design patterns
Contains authentication guides for multiple providers (Custom, Supabase, WorkOS)
Includes tutorials for tools, resources, prompts, and widgets implementation
Documents deployment strategies and best practices for production MCP servers
Offers common patterns and anti-patterns for MCP server development
Covers UI widget development with basics, interactivity, and state management
limitations: This is a reference/documentation skill, not an executable AI agent
Requires users to have programming knowledge to implement MCP servers
Does not generate code automatically - provides guidance and examples only
Examples are in TypeScript/Python - assumes familiarity with at least one language

use_cases.title: New MCP Server Development
use_cases.description: Developers starting a new MCP server project consult this skill for architecture guidance, authentication setup, and deployment best practices before writing code.
use_cases.target_user: Software developers new to MCP server development

use_cases.title: Debugging MCP Issues
use_cases.description: When encountering problems with existing MCP servers, developers reference this skill to identify common pitfalls and verify their implementation matches recommended patterns.
use_cases.target_user: Developers debugging existing MCP server implementations

use_cases.title: Learning MCP Concepts
use_cases.description: Engineers learning about Model Context Protocol use this skill to understand core concepts like tools, resources, prompts, and how they integrate with the mcp-use framework.

prompt_templates.title: Quick Start MCP Server
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me create a basic MCP server using the mcp-use framework. What are the essential files and structure I need?
prompt_templates.scenario: Starting a new MCP server project

prompt_templates.title: Add Authentication
prompt_templates.prompt: I need to add authentication to my MCP server. What authentication providers are supported and how do I configure them?

prompt_templates.title: Create Custom Tools
prompt_templates.prompt: Show me how to register custom tools in my MCP server. What is the correct pattern for tool definitions and handlers?

prompt_templates.title: Deploy to Production
prompt_templates.prompt: What are the best practices for deploying an MCP server to production? Include environment configuration and security considerations.

output_examples.input: How do I structure my MCP server?
output: A basic MCP server structure includes:
Each provider has specific configuration requirements documented in the references/authentication/ directory.

output_examples.input: How do I handle errors in my MCP server?
output: 1. Use response helpers for structured errors
4. Handle exceptions gracefully to prevent server crashes

best_practices: Always validate input parameters in tool handlers before processing
Use environment variables for sensitive configuration, never hardcode credentials
Register tools, resources, and prompts during server initialization for proper capability advertisement
Test MCP server locally using the MCP inspector before deployment
anti_patterns: Do not hardcode API keys or secrets in source code - use environment variables
Avoid blocking operations in tool handlers - use async patterns for I/O operations
Do not skip error handling - unhandled exceptions can crash the MCP server

faq.question: What is mcp-use?
faq.answer: mcp-use is a framework for building MCP (Model Context Protocol) servers in TypeScript and Python. It provides abstractions for tools, resources, prompts, and widgets.

faq.question: Which languages are supported?
faq.answer: mcp-use supports both TypeScript and Python for building MCP servers. The skill includes documentation for both language implementations.

faq.question: Do I need to install anything?
faq.answer: You will need to install the mcp-use library for your chosen language. The skill provides setup instructions and dependency information.

faq.question: Can this skill write code for me?
faq.answer: No, this skill is a reference guide. It provides documentation, patterns, and best practices. You implement the code following the guidance provided.

faq.question: What are widgets in MCP?
faq.answer: Widgets are interactive UI components that MCP servers can provide to Claude for rich user interactions. They include forms, lists, buttons, and other interface elements.

faq.question: How do I test my MCP server?
faq.answer: Use the MCP inspector tool to test your server locally before deployment. This allows you to verify tools, resources, and prompts work correctly.
